channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Russia pushed disinformation on social media about Hurricanes Milton and Helene
item.description: Russian actors pushed disinformation campaigns on social media around Hurricanes Milton and Helene in an attempt to sow distrust in the American government and stoke outrage over Ukrainian support ahead of November’s election, according to a new analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: F5, Nvidia team to boost AI, cloud security
item.description: F5 and Nvidia are expanding their partnership to help enterprises build AI infrastructure and bolster cloud-based application security.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI-powered weapons scanners used in NYC subway found zero guns in one month test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A pilot program testing AI-powered weapons scanners inside some New York City subway stations this summer did not detect any passengers with firearms — but falsely alerted more than 100 times, according to newly released police data.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google Cloud chief moves to ServiceNow as COO
item.description: Just a day after announcing his departure from Google to his LinkedIn followers, Amit Zavery said that he is joining ServiceNow as COO.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google's partnership with AI startup Anthropic faces a UK competition investigation
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s competition watchdog said Thursday it's opening a formal investigation into Google’s partnership with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michael Keaton: Trump, Musk don't respect supporters
item.description: Actor Michael Keaton said former President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk don’t respect their supporters, in a video posted to Instagram this week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work continues on JDK 24, with nine features so far proposed for the release: the generational Shenandoah garbage collector, scoped values, a key derivation function API, removal of the non-generational mode in the Z Garbage C
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New rules for US national security agencies balance AI's promise with need to protect against risks
item.description: New rules from the White House on the use of artificial intelligence by U.S. national security and spy agencies aim to balance the technology’s promise with the need to protect against its risks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Set up your smart speaker for emergencies
item.description: While you may already use your smart speaker for playing music, telling you the weather and answering trivia questions, there are also commands you can tell your device to get help in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS Leverages AI to Enhance Cybersecurity
item.description: Federal agencies are discovering the promise of artificial intelligence in dealing with cybersecurity data, but it's not a be-all and end-all solution.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Why enterprises should care more about net neutrality
item.description: Net neutrality is a topic that arouses nearly as many people as politics does, in no small part because it’s been a political issue. But nearly all of that emotion is at the consumer level. CIOs and network operations and planning professionals cite the topic as important in less than 5% of cases.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hydrogen startup Verdagy opens San Jose plant at risky moment
item.description: The Silicon Valley company is firing up its electrolyzer factory even as hydrogen demand has been slow to grow.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The best Python libraries for parallel processing
item.description: Python is powerful, versatile, and programmer-friendly, but it isn’t the fastest programming language around. Some of Python’s speed limitations are due to its default implementation, CPython, being single-threaded. That is, CPython doesn’t use more than one hardware thread at a time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service
item.description: The Social Security Administration is making IT investments that will make its employees more productive and its services more accessible to its customers. The post SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft to launch autonomous AI agents in November
item.description: Microsoft will soon let customers build autonomous AI agents that can be configured to perform complex tasks with little or no input from humans.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 11 open source AI projects that developers will love
item.description: The most creative and impactful ideas in software often appear as open source. Sharing code invites a global community of contributors to work together to drive a project forward. Artificial intelligence is no exception.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Recycling trucks with AI-powered cameras raise privacy concerns
item.description: The artificial intelligence can detect if there are any improperly recycled items and will log the information accordingly.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Phone bans in class wanted, but not all-day restrictions: Poll
item.description: 68% of U.S. adults said in a Pew Research Center survey conducted Sept. 30-Oct. 6, 2024 say they approve of such rules.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4
